task_completion.description: Verify at least 3 partition walls were added to the residential villa shell.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan view in the Sweet Home 3D interface. There should be at least 3 new interior wall segments drawn to divide the open space into separate areas.

task_completion.description: Verify at least 4 named rooms were created in the floor plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan view. There should be at least 4 distinct shaded room areas with visible text labels (e.g., 'Nap Room', 'Play Area', 'Kitchen', 'Restroom', 'Staff Office').

task_completion.description: Verify the placement of specific required furniture: 14+ chairs, 6+ tables, 6+ beds, 3+ kitchen appliances, 3+ toilets, 2+ sinks, and a desk with seating.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Examine the furniture list pane on the left and the 2D/3D views. You should see clusters of chairs and tables for activities, at least 6 beds in a designated nap area, kitchen appliances, bathroom fixtures (toilets/sinks), and an office desk.

task_completion.description: Verify the facility contains at least 40 total furniture items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the furniture list pane on the left side of the screen. The list should be heavily populated with items, or the 2D/3D views should look densely furnished, consistent with 40+ total objects.

task_completion.description: Verify at least 2 evacuation route polylines are drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan view. There should be at least two distinct lines or paths (polylines) drawn across the floor plan, originating from interior zones and leading toward the building exits.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show active interaction with the Sweet Home 3D application window (using tools, dragging furniture), rather than terminal commands or text editors modifying the file directly.

integrity.description: Verify the agent built the daycare center progressively.
integrity.visual_evidence: The sequence of screenshots should show a logical progression of adding walls, then rooms, then furniture, rather than a sudden jump from an empty shell to a fully completed design.

integrity.description: Verify the agent is working on the correct starter file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The title bar of the Sweet Home 3D application should read 'daycare_center_starter.sh3d'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying 'daycare_center_starter.sh3d' in Sweet Home 3D. The final design must include at least 3 partition walls, 4 named rooms (e.g., Nap Room, Play Area, Kitchen, Staff Office, Restroom), and at least 2 evacuation route polylines. Furniture requirements: >=14 chairs, >=6 tables, >=6 beds, >=3 kitchen appliances, >=3 toilets, >=2 sinks, >=1 desk with seating, and >=40 total furniture items. The pass threshold is 70/100 points.
